title: Reading and Use of English - Part 7
description: You are going to read an article about four people who discuss how technology affects their lives. For questions 43–52, choose from the people (A–D). The people may be chosen more than once.

content.title: Switched On: Four People on Technology in Daily Life

texts.A:
### Rachel, 26, Freelance Writer
[43]I work entirely online, so I depend on technology for absolutely everything – my income, my communication, my research.[/43] I wouldn't swap it for anything; the freedom to work from anywhere is wonderful. However, [47]I've noticed that I struggle to switch off in the evenings because there's always one more email to answer or one more article to finish.[/47] I recently started leaving my phone in a different room after 9 pm and it's made a real difference to my sleep. The challenge is that in a digital economy, being constantly available can feel like a professional necessity rather than a choice.
texts.B:
### Patrick, 62, Retired Bank Manager
[44]I was deeply sceptical of smartphones when they first appeared – I genuinely believed they would make people less capable of independent thought.[/44] I still have some of those concerns, but I've changed my view somewhat. [48]My grandchildren showed me how to video call, and now it's the highlight of my week when they live far away.[/48] I use a tablet for reading the news and managing my finances. [51]What I don't understand is why so many young people seem unable to have a conversation without checking their phone every few minutes.[/51] That kind of dependence worries me far more than the technology itself.
texts.C:
### Nadia, 34, Primary School Teacher
[45]I use technology extensively in the classroom – interactive boards, educational apps, and online quizzes – and I've seen it genuinely improve engagement.[/45] Children who used to stare out of the window now compete enthusiastically in vocabulary games. [49]My main concern is making sure screen time at school complements rather than replaces the hands-on activities that children also need.[/49] Balance is everything. [52]I also worry about children arriving at school exhausted because they've been on devices late at night, which is a growing problem I notice more and more each year.[/52]
texts.D:
### Marcos, 41, Architect
[46]Technology has completely transformed the way I design buildings – I now create detailed three-dimensional models that clients can actually walk through virtually before a single brick is laid.[/46] This saves enormous amounts of time and avoids costly misunderstandings. [50]The downside is that clients now expect changes to be made instantly, because they know the software can do it quickly, and that creates a lot of pressure.[/50] I also think something is lost when you go straight to the computer rather than starting with a pencil and paper. The hand-drawn sketch has a warmth and a looseness that no software has ever replicated.

questions.question: relies on technology completely for their work?
questions.explanation: Rachel says she depends on technology for everything – her income, communication and research.

questions.question: initially had a negative view of a particular type of technology?
questions.explanation: Patrick was deeply sceptical of smartphones when they first appeared.

questions.question: has seen technology have a positive effect on the people they work with?
questions.explanation: Nadia says using technology in the classroom has genuinely improved student engagement.

questions.question: explains how technology has changed the way they do their job?
questions.explanation: Marcos says technology has completely transformed how he designs buildings using 3D models.

questions.question: finds it hard to stop working at the end of the day because of technology?
questions.explanation: Rachel struggles to switch off in the evenings because there is always more work to do online.

questions.question: was introduced to a particular technology by a younger family member?
questions.explanation: Patrick's grandchildren showed him how to video call, which is now the highlight of his week.

questions.question: stresses the importance of not letting technology replace other valuable activities?
questions.explanation: Nadia emphasises that screen time should complement, not replace, hands-on activities for children.

questions.question: mentions that technology has raised expectations in a way that creates extra pressure?
questions.explanation: Marcos says clients now expect instant changes because they know the software can do it quickly.

questions.question: is critical of the way younger generations use technology socially?
questions.explanation: Patrick says he doesn't understand why young people seem unable to have a conversation without checking their phone.

questions.question: mentions a negative effect of technology on children's health or wellbeing?
questions.explanation: Nadia worries about children arriving at school exhausted from using devices late at night.
